semantic_deductions.question: Q19
semantic_deductions.reason: bias_model and bias_output are populated, but considerations.tradeoffs[] only addresses model-size vs accuracy/cost; it does not acknowledge accuracy-vs-fairness or rare-class performance tradeoffs implied by the disclosed biases.

semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Pipeline tag 'text-classification' ↔ output 'raw logits over {10 classes}' is consistent. Input encoded as SA-vocabulary is consistent with SaProt base model.

questions.name: Ethical & Responsible-AI Documentation
questions.score_label: Ethical considerations + bias disclosure + out-of-scope, all concrete
questions.evidence: considerations.ethical_considerations: 'Mis-localization in drug discovery pipelines' with concrete mitigation. bias_model and bias_output describe DeepLoc/SwissProt species bias and class-prior effects. out_of_scope_uses lists three specific scenarios.
questions.quality_note: Ethics + two bias dimensions + three out-of-scope items, all model-specific.

semantic_analysis.format_details: All ethics structures well-formed (named ethical consideration with mitigation_strategy; narrative bias fields; list-of-descriptions out_of_scope_uses).
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: sensitive_data_used is not flagged true (data.sensitive describes 'No human PII'), so the privacy-requirement cap rule does not trigger. The disclosed biases (species/class-prior) are concretely mirrored by out_of_scope_uses (underrepresented species; multi-label) — internal consistency holds. Model is not LLM/face/medical-imaging, so high-risk out-of-scope rule does not trigger.

questions.name: Training Procedure Clarity
questions.score_label: Optimizer + LR + batch + epochs + regularization disclosed
questions.evidence: hyperparameters: optimizer='AdamW', learning_rate=0.0005, batch_size=64, training_epochs=100, loss_function='Cross-entropy over 10 classes', optimization_techniques include LoRA rank/alpha/dropout, FP16, AdamW betas=(0.9, 0.98), weight_decay=0.01.
questions.quality_note: All required levers documented; LR schedule not stated, but regularization (weight_decay, LoRA dropout) covered.

semantic_analysis.format_details: Numeric hyperparameters typed correctly (float LR, int batch/epochs); LoRA rank/alpha/dropout values are within standard ranges.
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Hyperparameters in documentation prose match the structured hyperparameters block (AdamW, lr=5e-4, batch=64, epochs=100, FP16).
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: LR 5e-4 with AdamW for LoRA on a 650M base, batch 64, 100 epochs is within typical regimes for protein-LM fine-tuning.

questions.quality_note: Recognizable release tag, ISO date, meaningful change description.

semantic_analysis.format_details: version.name='1.0' is a recognizable release tag (semver-like MAJOR.MINOR); version.date='2024-08-01' is a valid ISO 8601 date.
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: version.date 2024-08-01 is not in the future (today is 2026-06-15) and does not predate the SaProt ICLR 2024 paper (which appeared earlier in 2024).

questions.name: Citations & References
questions.score_label: Multiple citations and multiple references with URLs (no DOIs)
questions.evidence: citations: 2 IEEE-style entries (SaProtHub HF page; Su et al. ICLR 2024 SaProt paper). references: 5 URLs (HF model, HF base model, GitHub SaProt, UniProt subcellular help, DeepLoc 2.0).
questions.quality_note: Two citations and five references, no DOIs and citations are not BibTeX.

semantic_analysis.format_details: Citations are well-formed IEEE strings with required 'style' key; not BibTeX, but YAML structure parses correctly. No DOIs in references.
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: The Su et al. citation acknowledges the base-model authors who are not in contributors[] but who are explicitly attributed via base_model and reference URLs — appropriate provenance.
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: ICLR 2024 timing is plausible for SaProt; both citation strings parse and are not broken YAML.

questions.score_label: No compute infrastructure section

semantic_analysis.format_details: Field absent; no value to format-check.
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: model_architecture references 650M-parameter base — a non-trivial scale — yet compute reporting is reduced to 'single GPU, FP16' in prose. Cap rule (Q15 cap at 3 for trivial compute report on large model) would apply, but baseline is already 0 due to wholly missing compute_infrastructure section, so no additional deduction.
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: LoRA fine-tuning of a 650M base for 100 epochs on a single GPU is plausible (LoRA is parameter-efficient), but the lack of total GPU-hours, GPU model, or energy figure prevents verification.
semantic_analysis.applied_cap: Large-model compute-scale rule would cap Q15 at 3; baseline already 0, so cap is not binding.

semantic_analysis.consistency_details: library_name='transformers' is consistent with SaProt's HF Transformers architecture; saprot_hub is a thin loader on top.

questions.name: Performance Metrics with Slices & CI
questions.score_label: One numeric metric, one slice, no CI
questions.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics=[{type: 'test accuracy', value: 85.75, slice: 'DeepLoc subcellular localization test set (10-way)', unit: '%'}]
questions.quality_note: Single accuracy value on a single slice; no per-class slices, no CI.

semantic_analysis.format_details: value=85.75 with unit='%' is in the [0, 100] valid range for accuracy.
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: The evaluation slice 'DeepLoc test set' is the held-out partition of the same DeepLoc dataset used for training (train split). This is standard train/test partitioning, not data leakage; the train-eval leakage cap rule does not trigger.
semantic_analysis.plausibility_details: 85.75% on DeepLoc 10-way subcellular localization is plausible and consistent with published SaProt-family benchmarks; not the >99% threshold that would trigger the suspicious-source cap.
semantic_analysis.applied_cap: Aggregate-only rule (no slice → cap at 3) is already binding; baseline Q18=3 already reflects this.

questions.name: Out-of-Scope Uses, Limitations & Tradeoffs
questions.score_label: All three populated, but tradeoffs do not address bias-implied accuracy-vs-fairness tension (capped from 5)
questions.evidence: limitations: 3 concrete items. tradeoffs: 1 item comparing 650M vs 35M variant accuracy/cost and LoRA trainable-parameter footprint. out_of_scope_uses: 3 specific scenarios.
questions.quality_note: All three sections populated, but the lone tradeoff is about model size; bias_model and bias_output (rare-class under-prediction, species bias) are not reflected in any acknowledged accuracy-vs-fairness tradeoff.

semantic_analysis.format_details: All three considerations sub-sections present with non-empty descriptions.
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: bias_model and bias_output are populated (species/class-prior bias). The semantic rule expects considerations.tradeoffs[] to acknowledge an accuracy-vs-fairness or aggregate-vs-rare-class tension. The only tradeoff documented is a parameter-count vs accuracy/inference-cost comparison between the 650M and 35M variants — it does not address the disclosed bias dimensions. Cap Q19 at 3.
semantic_analysis.applied_cap: Q19 capped at 3 because bias disclosures are not paired with an accuracy-vs-fairness tradeoff.

questions.name: Cross-Platform Interlinks
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: base_model='westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2' links to a HF Hub record; datasets=['SaProtHub/Dataset-Subcellular_Localization-DeepLoc'] links to a sibling HF dataset; references include the SaProt GitHub repo and DeepLoc 2.0 service.
questions.quality_note: Multiple cross-platform references.

semantic_analysis.format_details: base_model uses '{org}/{model}' shape; dataset id matches HF datasets convention.
semantic_analysis.consistency_details: Training dataset (SaProtHub mirror of DeepLoc) and evaluation slice (DeepLoc test set) share a common dataset family; train/test partition is the standard split, not leakage.

format_failures.issue: Value 'torch>=2.0' is a floating lower-bound, not a pinned MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH version. Triggers Q10/Q11 cap at 4, but baseline already lower so no additional deduction.

format_warnings.issue: Citations are IEEE-formatted strings rather than BibTeX entries. The rubric prefers BibTeX, but YAML structure is well-formed and citation 'style' key is populated; no cap applied.
References list only URLs; no DOIs included (e.g., SaProt ICLR paper and DeepLoc 2.0 paper both have DOIs that could be cited).
'1.0' is a recognizable release tag but not a full MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH semver triple. Accepted under the recognizable-release-tag clause; no cap applied.

consistency_failures.rule: bias_model/bias_output populated → tradeoffs must acknowledge accuracy-vs-fairness
consistency_failures.issue: bias_model (species/family bias) and bias_output (rare-class under-prediction for peroxisome/plastid) are populated, but considerations.tradeoffs[] only covers model size (650M vs 35M) vs accuracy and inference cost. There is no documented tradeoff addressing aggregate accuracy vs rare-compartment performance or fairness across underrepresented species.

consistency_failures.rule: Large model scale → non-trivial compute_infrastructure expected
consistency_failures.issue: model_architecture describes a 650M-parameter base, but model_parameters.compute_infrastructure is absent and the only compute mention is 'single GPU, FP16' in prose. Cap rule would set Q15 at 3, but baseline already 0; cap is not the binding constraint.

consistency_passes.note: pipeline_tag='text-classification' is consistent with output 'raw logits over 10 classes'.
SaProt_650M_AF2 (MIT upstream) and MIT downstream are compatible.
DeepLoc test slice is the held-out partition of the DeepLoc training set; standard train/test split, not leakage.
sensitive_data field documents 'No human PII; SwissProt protein sequences only'; sensitive_data_used flag is not asserted true, so privacy-requirement rule does not trigger.
Protein localization classifier is not in the high-risk list (LLM/face/medical-imaging/surveillance); rule does not trigger. out_of_scope_uses is nevertheless populated.

plausibility_passes.note: test accuracy 85.75% is within [0, 100] and consistent with published SaProt-family benchmarks on DeepLoc.
2024-08-01 is not in the future (today: 2026-06-15) and does not predate the cited SaProt ICLR 2024 paper.
AdamW lr=5e-4, batch=64, 100 epochs, FP16 with LoRA rank=16/alpha=32 is a typical regime for protein-LM LoRA fine-tuning.

strengths: Complete structural metadata: all required fields, eight tags, pipeline_tag, language, model_category, and full I/O specification including format maps and class interpretation.
Strong responsible-AI block: concrete ethical concern with mitigation, both bias_model and bias_output narratives, three model-specific out-of-scope statements, and three concrete limitations.
Comprehensive training-procedure disclosure: optimizer, LR, batch size, epochs, loss, LoRA rank/alpha/dropout, weight decay, AdamW betas, and mixed-precision setting.
Format-correct identifiers: MIT is a valid SPDX id; HF Hub URLs follow the {org}/{model} convention; version date is valid ISO 8601; accuracy value is in the [0, 100] range.
Good cross-platform interlinking via base_model, datasets, GitHub, and DeepLoc references.
weaknesses: Tradeoffs do not acknowledge the accuracy-vs-fairness tension implied by the disclosed bias_model and bias_output (semantic cap on Q19 from 5 to 3).
compute_infrastructure section is absent; with a 650M base, total GPU-hours and energy/carbon should be documented (Q15 = 0).
framework_version='torch>=2.0' is a floating lower bound rather than a pinned release, and no model_index is provided (Q10, Q11 = 3).
Performance reporting is thin: one accuracy on one aggregate slice, no per-class breakdown or confidence intervals (Q18 = 3).
Citations are IEEE strings rather than BibTeX, and references list URLs but no DOIs (Q14 = 4).
recommendations: Add a tradeoff statement that explicitly pairs the bias_model/bias_output disclosures with the accuracy-vs-rare-class or aggregate-vs-fairness tension (this alone would restore Q19 toward 5).
Add a model_parameters.compute_infrastructure section with GPU model and count, total GPU-hours, and an energy or carbon estimate.
Disclose the funder, grant ID, and any computing facility (e.g., the Westlake cluster) that supported training.
Expand performance_metrics with per-compartment accuracy / macro-F1 and bootstrap 95% confidence intervals so rare-class performance is visible.
Pin framework_version to an exact PyTorch release (e.g., torch==2.1.0) and add a Papers-with-Code-style model_index entry or Dockerfile/lockfile reference.
Promote references to DOIs where available (SaProt ICLR paper, DeepLoc 2.0 paper) and convert citations to BibTeX entries.
